name: Taylor Swift
description: From country debut to 'Eras Tour' megastar — Swift's albums, songs, and record-breaking career.

questions.q: In which U.S. state was Taylor Swift born?

source.notes: Born December 13, 1989 in West Reading, PA.

questions.q: Which album title doubles as Taylor Swift's birth year?

source.notes: Released October 2014; her first pop album.

questions.q: What was the title of Taylor Swift's debut single, released in 2006?

source.notes: Named after the country star.

questions.q: For which song's Best Female Video win did Kanye West interrupt Taylor Swift's 2009 VMA acceptance speech?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: removed incorrect '1989 album' framing ('You Belong with Me' is from Fearless, 2008) and dropped awkward trick-question wording. Kanye said 'Beyoncé had one of the best videos of all time.'

questions.q: Which of these albums is Taylor Swift's debut self-titled country record?

source.notes: Released October 24, 2006.

questions.q: Which 2012 album produced hits like 'We Are Never Ever Getting Back Together' and '22'?

source.notes: A transitional album — country fading into pop.

questions.q: In 2020, Taylor Swift surprise-released which pair of indie-folk sister albums?

source.notes: Folklore (July 2020) and Evermore (December 2020).

questions.q: Who is the primary collaborator/producer on most of Taylor's post-1989 pop albums?

source.notes: Antonoff has co-produced every album since 1989; Aaron Dessner did Folklore/Evermore.

questions.q: Which 2008 album was Taylor Swift's breakthrough, winning Album of the Year at the 2010 Grammys?

source.notes: Made her the youngest-ever AOTY winner at 20.

questions.q: Which 2023-2024 tour grossed over $2 billion, becoming the highest-grossing concert tour ever?

source.notes: Passed $1B in December 2023; ended 2024 at $2B+.

questions.q: In 2021, what did Taylor Swift begin releasing — re-recorded versions of her older albums — in a fight to own her masters?

source.notes: Fearless (Taylor's Version) was the first, April 2021.

questions.q: Taylor Swift's album 'Midnights' was released in which year?

source.notes: Released October 21, 2022.

questions.q: Which song first took Taylor to #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2014?
choices: We Are Never Ever Getting Back Together

source.notes: Shake It Off reached #1 in August 2014.

questions.q: Which rapper's feud with Swift was revived by a leaked 2016 phone call concerning the song 'Famous'?

source.notes: The feud originated with his 2009 VMAs interruption.

questions.q: Which 10-minute version of an older song did Taylor release on Red (Taylor's Version)?

source.notes: The '10 Minute Version' topped the Hot 100.

questions.q: Which NFL tight end did Taylor begin dating in 2023, intensifying her presence at games?

source.notes: Kansas City Chiefs tight end.

questions.q: In which 2019 musical-film adaptation did Taylor Swift play Bombalurina?

source.notes: Directed by Tom Hooper; she also wrote 'Beautiful Ghosts' for the film.

questions.q: Which country legend was Taylor Swift named after?

source.notes: Her parents admired James Taylor; 'Taylor' works for any gender.

questions.q: The Taylor Swift-starring concert film of the Eras Tour was distributed by which studio (bypassing studio-label economics)?

source.notes: Cut out traditional studios; grossed $250M+ worldwide.

questions.q: In 2024, Taylor Swift released a surprise double album also called what?

source.notes: 'The Tortured Poets Department' released April 19, 2024, with an 'Anthology' expanded edition the same day.

questions.q: Taylor Swift has won Album of the Year at the Grammys a record how many times (through 2024)?

source.notes: Fearless (2010), 1989 (2016), Folklore (2021), Midnights (2024) — most AOTY wins ever.
